---
title: Population growth is concentrated in Nonrural areas
description: Line chart showing population change since the Great Recession for rural
  and nonrural areas.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-pop-change-since-gr-lc/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Demographics
  - Rural Development
tags:
  - PEP
  - Population
  - Rural vs. Nonrural
  - County-Level
  - Line Chart
image: 3.3_pop_change_since_GR_lc.svg
chart:
  title: Population growth is concentrated in Nonrural areas
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  dataSource: U.S. Census Bureau, Population Estimates Program
  sourceUrl: https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/popest.html
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - census-population-estimates
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This chart tracks population changes in rural vs nonrural counties since 2007, revealing continued urbanization trends.](./3.3_pop_change_since_GR_lc.svg)

## Overview

This chart tracks population changes in rural vs nonrural counties since 2007, revealing continued urbanization trends.

## Key Findings

- Nonrural areas have experienced steady population growth since 2007
- Rural population has remained largely flat with slight decline in some periods
- The pandemic briefly reversed urban growth trends

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/population_lc.R` in the producing project.
